meaning: To happen, to occur, to come to pass
definition: To take place or occur, typically as an unforeseen event or historic happenstance.
sample_sentence: Nadie pudo prever los fallos que iban a acaecer en el servidor. (No one could foresee the failures that were to happen on the server.)

definition: Pure, refined, and free from any imperfections or moral flaws.
sample_sentence: El equipo trabaja con un acendrado compromiso con la calidad. (The team works with a spotless commitment to quality.)

meaning: To intimidate, to daunt, to cow
definition: To frighten or intimidate someone, causing them to lose courage or confidence.
sample_sentence: No nos dejamor acoquinar por las dificultades del desarrollo. (We do not let ourselves be intimidated by development difficulties.)

definition: Excessively complicated, refined, or distorted, making it difficult to comprehend.
sample_sentence: Evita el uso de código alambicado en las funciones de renderizado. (Avoid using overly intricate code in the rendering functions.)

definition: Unable to bend, adapt, or progress due to outmoded rules or structural rigidity.
sample_sentence: Un sistema operativo anquilosado no procesará bien los Pointer Events. (A stagnant operating system will not process Pointer Events well.)

definition: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern toward a dynamic context or action.
sample_sentence: La apatía del estudiante desaparece gracias a la gamificación. (The student's apathy disappears thanks to gamification.)

definition: To catch a brief or partial view of something, or perceive it indistinctly.
sample_sentence: Ya podemos atisbar el éxito de la nueva arquitectura de sandbox. (We can already glimpse the success of the new sandbox architecture.)

definition: A change of circumstances or fortune, especially one that is unwelcome or volatile.
sample_sentence: Sobrevivimos a los avatares del mercado gracias a una base sólida. (We survived the vicissitudes of the market thanks to a solid foundation.)

definition: The condition or fact of being unoriginal, obvious, or completely boring.
sample_sentence: Debemos alejarnos de la banalidad en los métodos de enseñanza. (We must move away from triviality in teaching methods.)

definition: Hostile, aggressive, and eager to engage in arguments or functional combat.
sample_sentence: El foro adoptó una postura beligerante ante el cambio de licencia. (The forum adopted a belligerent stance toward the license change.)

meaning: To struggle, to toil, to grapple
definition: To work hard or struggle continuously against technical difficulties or obstacles.
sample_sentence: Tuvimos que bregar con las restricciones de Safari durante días. (We had to grapple with Safari's restrictions for days.)

definition: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds occurring simultaneously in a system output.
sample_sentence: El fallo de audio generó una cacofonía molesta en los auriculares. (The audio failure generated an annoying cacophony in the headphones.)

definition: Calculated to confuse, entrap, or deceive, especially in a legal or logical argument.
sample_sentence: Las preguntas capciosas ayudan a evaluar el razonamiento profundo. (Tricky questions help to evaluate deep reasoning.)

definition: The suppression or prohibition of speech or code, or an expression of strong disapproval.
sample_sentence: El código malicioso se enfrentó a la censura del cortafuegos. (The malicious code faced the censorship of the firewall.)

definition: Exaggerated or aggressive patriotism, or an irrational belief in national superiority.
sample_sentence: Debemos evitar el chovinismo tecnológico al elegir herramientas. (We must avoid tech chauvinism when choosing tools.)

meaning: To give in, to back down, to yield
definition: To give up or yield to pressure, or abandon a technical standard under duress.
sample_sentence: El ingeniero se negó a claudicar ante las prisas del despliegue. (The engineer refused to back down despite the deployment rush.)

definition: The mental action or process of acquiring knowledge and understanding through experiences.
sample_sentence: El juego interactivo acelera los procesos de cognición lingüística. (Interactive play accelerates linguistic cognition processes.)

definition: An opinion or conclusion formed on the basis of incomplete database tracking logs.
sample_sentence: Eso es solo una conjetura; necesitamos métricas reales de producción. (That is just a conjecture; we need real production metrics.)

meaning: Commensurable, measurable by a common standard
definition: Measurable or capable of being compared by a uniform parameter scale.
sample_sentence: El rendimiento de los micro-juegos no es conmesurable con el de apps web pesadas. (The performance of micro-games is not commensurable with that of heavy web apps.)

definition: The action of wasting away or slowly consuming resources or processing power.
sample_sentence: La consunción de memoria ram arruinó la sesión de juego. (The consumption of RAM ruined the gaming session.)

definition: Dependent on a current combination of temporary circumstances rather than permanent design.
sample_sentence: El pico de tráfico fue un problema coyuntural, no estructural. (The traffic spike was a situational problem, not structural.)

definition: Very generous, especially regarding gifts, funds, or open-source support.
sample_sentence: El patrocinador fue muy dadivoso con los incentivos de la comunidad. (The sponsor was very generous with the community incentives.)

definition: Morally contemptible, or physically fragile and easily destroyed.
sample_sentence: El código heredado tenía una estructura deleznable que fallaba siempre. (The legacy code had a brittle structure that failed every time.)

meaning: To vilify, to revile, to insult
definition: To abuse or criticize someone or a technical methodology very harshly in public.
sample_sentence: No se debe denostar el uso de archivos JSON locales en etapas iniciales. (One should not vilify using local JSON files in early stages.)

definition: A complete lack of care, effort, or professional diligence in execution.
sample_sentence: La desidia en la seguridad de datos puede provocar hackeos graves. (Negligence in data security can lead to severe hacks.)

definition: Lacking brilliance, sparkle, or excitement; looking poorly optimized.
sample_sentence: El rendimiento de la app móvil quedó deslucido por los fallos de scroll. (The mobile app's performance was rendered lacklustre by scroll bugs.)

definition: An easy, confident, and slightly bold manner in communication or design styles.
sample_sentence: El maverick del software presentó sus ideas con mucho desparpajo. (The software maverick presented his ideas with a lot of self-confidence.)

definition: Lacking distinct color, sharpness, or structural contrast; pale.
sample_sentence: La paleta visual del juego utiliza tonos desvaídos de estilo retro. (The visual palette of the game uses faded tones of a retro style.)

definition: A division or contrast between two things that are entirely opposite or different.
sample_sentence: Existe una dicotomía clara entre el renderizado DOM y Canvas. (There is a clear dichotomy between DOM and Canvas rendering.)

definition: A great difference or inequality between system parameters or metrics.
sample_sentence: Vimos una disparidad enorme entre las métricas locales y las de producción. (We saw a huge disparity between local and production metrics.)

definition: Deriving ideas, styles, or technical methods from a broad and diverse range of sources.
sample_sentence: El portal web tiene un estilo ecléctico que combina arcade y minimalismo. (The web portal has an eclectic style that combines arcade and minimalism.)

definition: Expressing feelings of gratitude, approval, or pleasure in a heartfelt manner.
sample_sentence: El equipo recibió una felicitación efusiva del director ejecutivo. (The team received an effusive congratulations from the CEO.)

definition: Fluent, persuasive, and elegant speaking or writing quality.
sample_sentence: Su elocuencia convenció a los inversores sobre el valor de la app web. (Her eloquence convinced investors about the value of the web app.)

meaning: To evade, to elude, to dodge
definition: To escape from or avoid a legal, technical, or structural obligation via skill.
sample_sentence: La arquitectura modular nos permite eludir errores en tiempo de ejecución. (The modular architecture allows us to evade errors at runtime.)

meaning: To trick, to con, to bamboozle
definition: To cheat or deceive someone, especially by using flattering words or false metrics.
sample_sentence: Es imposible embaucar al auditor con datos de tráfico falsos. (It is impossible to trick the auditor with fake traffic data.)

definition: Deep-seated ill will, bitterness, or long-lasting technical acrimony.
sample_sentence: El debate sobre los frameworks terminó con cierto encono en el foro. (The debate over frameworks ended with some bitterness on the forum.)

definition: Regularly found and deeply rooted within a specific area, code repository, or culture.
sample_sentence: Los fallos de latencia de entrada eran un problema endémico de la versión antigua. (Input latency bugs were an endemic problem of the old version.)

definition: The most important, essential, or substantive part of an argument or technical piece.
sample_sentence: Es un döküman de gran enjundia técnica para los desarrolladores. (It is a document of great technical substance for developers.)

definition: Extremely complex, messy, and hard to unravel or organize properly.
sample_sentence: Tuvimos que reescribir un código de estilos CSS enmarañado. (We had to rewrite a tangled CSS styles code.)

definition: The structural network, framework, or core grid system that holds an enterprise architecture.
sample_sentence: El entramado de microservicios funciona de forma asíncrona. (The microservices framework works asynchronously.)

definition: A thing with distinct and independent existence within a system or database.
sample_sentence: Cada par de idiomas se guarda como un ente independiente en el JSON. (Each language pair is saved as an independent entity in the JSON.)

definition: Lasting for a very brief or transient moment of execution time.
sample_sentence: Las variables locales tienen un ciclo de vida efímero en la memoria. (Local variables have an ephemeral lifecycle in memory.)

definition: Mental calmness and composure, especially when managing a massive production crash.
sample_sentence: El DevOps lideró la restauración del servicio con total equanimidad. (The DevOps led the service restoration with absolute equanimity.)

definition: Deep and extensive knowledge acquired through diligent study and research fields.
sample_sentence: La erudición del lingüista ayudó a clasificar las frases complejas. (The linguist's erudition helped classify the complex phrases.)

definition: A highly grotesque, absurd, or poorly managed technical or visual situation.
sample_sentence: El diseño de la interfaz web era un esperpento antes del rediseño. (The design of the web interface was a grotesque absurdity before the redesign.)

definition: Intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of technical experts.
sample_sentence: Utiliza algoritmos esotéricos que solo él comprende completamente. (He uses esoteric algorithms that only he fully understands.)

definition: The sound or sign marking the absolute end or collapse of a technical system lifecycle.
sample_sentence: Esos errores de timeout son el estertor del servidor heredado. (Those timeout errors are the death rattle of the legacy server.)

definition: To make a technical problem, error statement, or memory leak much worse.
sample_sentence: Añadir más animaciones DOM solo va a exacerbar el lag en móviles. (Adding more DOM animations will only exacerbate lag on mobiles.)

definition: Extremely small in size, amount, or available system memory resource.
sample_sentence: El dispositivo tiene un espacio de almacenamiento exiguo. (The device has a meager storage space.)

meaning: To plunder, to despoil, to strip
definition: To strip, plunder, or extract valuable technical assets illegally from a database context.
sample_sentence: Los hackers intentaron expoliar los tokens confidenciales. (The hackers tried to plunder the confidential tokens.)

definition: A mistaken belief or flaw in logic, especially one founded on unsound technical arguments.
sample_sentence: Creer que más líneas de código significan más calidad es una falacia. (Believing that more lines of code mean higher quality is a fallacy.)

definition: Silly, pointless, or arrogantly self-satisfied in structural tech deployment paths.
sample_sentence: El experto dio un discurso fatuo que no aportó soluciones reales. (The expert gave a fatuous speech that provided no real solutions.)

definition: Intense, passionate, and eager energy focused toward an objective goal or pro.
sample_sentence: La comunidad adoptó el juego educativo con un fervor increíble. (The community adopted the educational game with incredible fervor.)

definition: A formal link, connection, or structural tracing back to a source object parent.
sample_sentence: Debemos comprobar la filiación de los componentes de código abierto. (We must check the affiliation of the open-source components.)

definition: Lack of firm structure, tautness, or logical precision inside an architecture build.
sample_sentence: La flaccidez de las políticas de QA provocó fugas de código. (The laxity of QA policies caused code leaks.)

definition: Lack of serious purpose, value, or architectural depth; lighthearted silliness.
sample_sentence: No trates la seguridad del backend con frivolidad. (Do not treat backend security with frivolity.)

definition: Intense, dazzling brightness or outstanding execution brilliance of an asset layout.
sample_sentence: El nuevo motor de canvas devolvió el fulgor gráfico a los arcades. (The new canvas engine brought graphic brilliance back to the arcades.)

definition: Done in a quiet, stealthy, or hidden manner to avoid technical trace detection.
sample_sentence: El virus realizó una descarga furtiva de archivos ejecutables. (The virus performed a furtive download of executable files.)

definition: Excessively talkative, wordy, or producing unneeded log bloat parameters.
sample_sentence: El script de monitoreo es demasiado gárrulo y llena el disco de logs vacíos. (The monitoring script is too garrulous and fills the disk with empty logs.)

definition: The scale or level of detail in a set of data metrics or permissions logic.
sample_sentence: El sistema permite ajustar la granularidad de los datos del usuario. (The system allows adjusting the granularity of user data.)

definition: Bored, jaded, or spent due to excessive exposure to long repetitive processes.
sample_sentence: El programador está hastiado de solucionar fallos de compatibilidad CSS. (The programmer is weary of fixing CSS compatibility bugs.)

definition: Leadership or absolute dominance, especially by one country, tech stack, or engine.
sample_sentence: Chromium mantiene su hegemonía en el mercado mundial de navegadores. (Chromium maintains its hegemony in the global browser market.)

definition: The quality of craftsmanship, execution method, or custom build processing.
sample_sentence: El panel de control es de una hechura impecable y modular. (The control panel is of an unblemished and modular workmanship.)

definition: A pause, gap, or structural interruption in a continuous technical series loop.
sample_sentence: Hubo un hiato en el desarrollo antes de iniciar la fase de Firebase. (There was a hiatus in development before starting the Firebase phase.)

definition: Extreme amusement, laughter, or funny appreciation of an edge case layout error.
sample_sentence: El bug del avatar flotante causó mucha hilaridad en el equipo de QA. (The floating avatar bug caused much hilarity in the QA team.)

definition: A person who attacks traditional tech norms or established software patterns.
sample_sentence: Su rechazo a usar frameworks tradicionales lo define como un iconoclasta. (His rejection of traditional frameworks defines him as an iconoclast.)

definition: A distinct architectural quirk or behavioral feature unique to an asset engine.
sample_sentence: Debemos adaptarnos a la idiosincrasia de iOS Safari para evitar bloqueos. (We must adapt to the idiosyncrasy of iOS Safari to avoid crashes.)

definition: Public shame, absolute technical failure, or deep professional disgrace.
sample_sentence: El robo masivo de datos fue una ignominia para el proveedor de la nube. (The massive data breach was an ignominy for the cloud provider.)

definition: Incapable of being upset, worried, or technical processing cycle disrupted.
sample_sentence: La base de datos se mantuvo imperturbable durante los picos de peticiones. (The database remained imperturbable during request peaks.)

definition: Never fading, imperishable, or retaining structural value indefinitely.
sample_sentence: La importancia de escribir código limpio es una ley inmarcesible. (The importance of writing clean code is an everlasting law.)

definition: Impossible to stop, alter, or alter via external technical intervention tools.
sample_sentence: El avance hacia los sistemas de IA generativa es inexorable. (The advance toward generative AI systems is inexorable.)

definition: Grossly unfair, harmful, or technically unjust in access permissions setup.
sample_sentence: La plataforma impuso un reparto de ingresos inicuo para los creadores. (The platform imposed an unfair revenue share for creators.)

definition: Impossible to understand, parse, or decode properly by a system compiler.
sample_sentence: El archivo de configuración quedó corrupto e ininteligible. (The configuration file became corrupt and unintelligible.)

definition: Never fading, imperishable, or retaining structural value indefinitely.
sample_sentence: La importancia de escribir código limpio es una ley inmarcesible. (The importance of writing clean code is an everlasting law.)

definition: A state of complete ignorance or cluelessness regarding a factual context.
sample_sentence: El analista estaba en la inopia respecto a los nuevos cambios de SEO. (The analyst was completely clueless regarding the new SEO changes.)

definition: Showing an analytical, deeply curious, or questioning mind toward bugs.
sample_sentence: El programador principal tiene un enfoque inquisitivo al revisar logs. (The lead programmer has an inquisitive approach when reviewing logs.)

definition: Impossible to measure, calculate, or comprehend completely due to its depth.
sample_sentence: La cantidad de datos no estructurados en la web es insondable. (The amount of unstructured data on the web is unfathomable.)

definition: An intervening space, gap, or crack between structural system modules.
sample_sentence: El script encontró un intersticio de seguridad en la validación del backend. (The script found a security gap in the backend validation.)

definition: Insulting, critical, or highly abusive language used to attack a tech choice.
sample_sentence: Su artículo fue una invectiva dura contra los monopolios tecnológicos. (His article was a harsh invective against tech monopolies.)

definition: With good judgment, logic, or sense; wisely coordinating system variables.
sample_sentence: Debes asignar los recursos de computación juiciosamente. (You must allocate computing resources judiciously.)

definition: The act or instance of placing independent system components close together for review.
sample_sentence: La yuxtaposición de los dos frameworks facilitó la elección del equipo. (The juxtaposition of the two frameworks made the team's choice easier.)

definition: The quality of using very few words; briefness and conciseness in logs or docs.
sample_sentence: El laconismo de los informes de error retrasó la depuración del shell. (The conciseness of the error reports delayed debugging the shell.)

definition: Non-expert or secular, not belonging to a specialized expert group tier.
sample_sentence: Explicar el renderizado WebGL a un público laico es todo un reto. (Explaining WebGL rendering to a lay audience is quite a challenge.)

definition: Excessive or lustful indulgence, or over-elaborate ornamentation in visual style.
sample_sentence: El estilo barroco del juego rozaba la lascivia visual. (The baroque style of the game bordered on visual over-indulgence.)

definition: Extreme care, precision, and thorough attention to technical detail guidelines.
sample_sentence: La meticulosidad al revisar el JSON evitó errores de formato en Firebase. (Meticulousness when reviewing the JSON avoided formatting errors in Firebase.)

definition: An obsession with creating false stories or reporting distorted metric values.
sample_sentence: Ciertas startups caen en la mitomanía al anunciar millones de descargas. (Certain startups fall into mythomania when announcing millions of downloads.)

definition: The lowest point in the fortunes, stability, or traffic metrics of an organization.
sample_sentence: El rendimiento del sistema llegó a su nadir el día del fallo de red. (System performance reached its nadir on the day of the network failure.)

definition: Utterly wicked, heinous, or detestable code injection or exploit tactics.
sample_sentence: El ataque de denegación de servicio fue un acto nefando. (The denial of service attack was a nefarious act.)

definition: A connection or central link binding two separate system components together.
sample_sentence: El postMessage es el nexo de unión entre el host y el shell. (The postMessage is the link between the host and the shell.)

meaning: To cloud, to obfuscate, to dazzle
definition: To cloud, confuse, or obscure the logical clarity of an interface or mind.
sample_sentence: Demasiadas alertas visuales pueden obnubilar la mente del usuario. (Too many visual alerts can cloud the user's mind.)

definition: A wavy movement or smooth continuous curve layout inside graphic transitions.
sample_sentence: El canvas simula una ondulación perfecta del agua en la pantalla. (The canvas simulates a perfect undulation of water on the screen.)

definition: Public disgrace or strong criticism arising from bad technical execution behavior.
sample_sentence: La pérdida de datos privados trajo el oprobio sobre la empresa de hosting. (The loss of private data brought opprobrium upon the hosting company.)

definition: Decoration or excessive flourish added to an interface, often slowing down speeds.
sample_sentence: Prescinde de cualquier ornato innecesario para optimizar la carga móvil. (Dispense with any unnecessary adornment to optimize mobile load.)

definition: Apparent, clearly visible, or manifest on the surface layout of an object.
sample_sentence: El cambio de rendimiento fue ostensible tras aislar el CSS. (The performance change was manifest after isolating the CSS.)

definition: The condition of being a perfect, clear archetype or model pattern for a system.
sample_sentence: El paradigmatismo de este PRD servirá de base para futuros desarrollos. (The paradigmatic nature of this PRD will serve as a basis for future developments.)

definition: Extreme care or slowness in action, or resource expenditure thrift.
sample_sentence: El motor carga los archivos multimedia con calculada parsimonia. (The engine loads multimedia files with calculated slowness.)

definition: Reasonable or probable; having a sound basis in logic or fact configuration.
sample_sentence: Esa es una explicación plausible para los fallos del puntero en iOS. (That is a plausible explanation for the pointer failures on iOS.)

definition: The philosophical view that the self is all that can be verified to exist.
sample_sentence: El solipsismo técnico ignora por completo el feedback real de los usuarios. (Technical solipsism completely ignores real user feedback.)

definition: Aggressive, defiant behavior, or a grimly harsh visual content presentation.
sample_sentence: El juego de terror moderó su truculencia visual para las tiendas de apps. (The horror game moderated its visual truculence for app stores.)

definition: The most powerful, successful, or highest point of systemic execution development.
sample_sentence: Memolandum alcanzará su zenit cuando consolide todos sus pares de idiomas. (Memolandum will reach its zenith when it consolidates all its language pairs.)
